strengths: Experience with ETL and data pipelines
Proven reporting and dashboard builds (Power BI, Tableau)
Quantified impact on operations and acquisition
weaknesses: No explicit experience with Snowflake/BigQuery in role
improvement_bullets: Integrated diverse data sources, including survey and assessment-related data, into unified PostgreSQL schemas to support clean, reliable reporting for organizational stakeholders.
Managed data intake and fulfillment requests using JIRA as a primary ticketing system, ensuring accurate tracking and timely delivery of data to cross-functional teams.
Executed end-to-end data pipelines using Python and SQL to query data from cloud environments, including BigQuery and Snowflake, for large-scale analytical projects.
Leveraged scripting techniques in Python to automate data cleaning and validation, improving data quality and reducing manual entry errors for recurring reporting tasks.
Presented data-driven insights through compelling reports and documentation, utilizing visualization tools and slide decks to communicate outcomes to leadership and non-technical staff.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Temporary Data Request Analyst opportunity with Uncommon Schools. I am interested in this opportunity because I am deeply inspired by your commitment to closing the college completion gap and providing an exceptional K-12 education in economically disadvantaged communities. What interests me about working for Uncommon Schools is your focus on investing deeply in educators and students, which resonates with my belief that data should be used to empower people and improve systemic outcomes. I believe my technical proficiency in SQL and Python and my experience in cross-functional stakeholder support would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on efficiency and precision. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in client onboarding and implemented an automated workflow that saved 8 hours per week, which allowed the team to focus on high-impact customer acquisition initiatives. By streamlining this data intake process, I increased team efficiency by 25%. Ultimately, I know that the Temporary Data Request Analyst role at Uncommon Schools would allow me to bring my data cleaning and modeling skills to the vital task of supporting academic and development teams with actionable insights. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my ticketing and collaborative documentation skills using JIRA and Confluence. In that role, I managed and reconciled complex insurance testing data, which reduced project delivery delays by 25%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in managing data requests and documentation in working on reporting and process improvement at Uncommon Schools. I know that my technical versatility and commitment to data accuracy align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
